title: PbtTestPlan
description: A property-based test plan for one function. The model decides whether any property holds and, if so, selects invariants from a catalog. Code rendering is done deterministically by the host, not by the model.

decision.description: 'generate' when at least one property holds; 'skip' when no general property holds for any input.

skipReason.description: Required when decision is 'skip'. A short explanation of why no property applies.

invariants.description: Required when decision is 'generate'. One entry per property that holds.

kind.description: Which property from the catalog. Use 'custom' only when nothing else fits.

testName.description: Human-readable description of the property, e.g. 'reverse is its own inverse'.

args.description: Exact parameter names from the signature that this property binds to, in order.

value.description: For 'identity_element': the identity literal as Kotlin source, e.g. "0" or "\"\"".

predicate.description: For 'output_constraint': a Kotlin Boolean expression over `result` and parameter names, e.g. 'result >= 0'.

reference.description: For 'oracle': a Kotlin expression over the parameters computing the expected value independently of the function under test.

code.description: For 'custom' ONLY: a Kotlin assertion body executed inside checkAll with the parameters and `result` in scope.
